ba.label: BA / single beetle behavioral assay
ba.description: One-beetle post-processing for IDtracker.ai output folders. Produces per-frame kinematics, individual summary CSVs, QC maps, ROI/wall-buffer summaries, interpolation/artifact summaries, and turtling-like event summaries.

analysis_window_frames.label: Analysis window length, in frames
analysis_window_frames.help: Number of frames analyzed after the analysis start frame. Default is 7500.

fps.label: Video frames per second
fps.help: Used only to convert frame counts to seconds for event files and convenience columns.

animal_index.label: Animal index
animal_index.help: Zero-based animal index for BA sessions. Usually 0.

move_threshold_px.label: Movement threshold, pixels
move_threshold_px.help: Displacement/cumulative movement threshold used for movement onset metrics.

movement_onset_consecutive_frames.label: Sustained movement frames
movement_onset_consecutive_frames.help: Number of consecutive frames above the movement threshold required for sustained movement onset.

max_step_px.label: Artifact jump threshold, pixels
max_step_px.help: One-frame jumps larger than this are treated as likely tracking artifacts and interpolated if bounded by valid positions.

roi_wall_buffer_px.label: ROI wall buffer, pixels
roi_wall_buffer_px.help: Distance from the primary ROI wall used to classify wall/border occupancy.

turtling_window_frames.label: Turtling scan window, frames
turtling_window_frames.help: Window size, in tracking frames, for centroid-based turtling-like detection. In 1 fps timelapse data, 300 frames is about 300 seconds.

turtling_min_duration_frames.label: Minimum turtling duration, frames
turtling_min_duration_frames.help: Minimum run length, in tracking frames, required to retain a turtling-like event. In 1 fps timelapse data, 300 frames is about 300 seconds.

metadata_notes: Analysis start frame is read from each cell TOML and stored as toml_analysis_start_frame in the manifest.

fight.label: Fight / two beetle combat assay
fight.description: Two-beetle combat post-processing for IDtracker.ai output folders. Produces pairwise distance/contact/fight-like event summaries plus individual BA-style metrics for both beetles.

analysis_window_frames.label: Analysis window length, in frames
analysis_window_frames.help: Number of frames analyzed after the analysis start frame. Default is 7500.

fps.label: Video frames per second
fps.help: Used to convert frame counts to seconds for events and InqScribe files.

animal0.label: Animal 0 index
animal0.help: Zero-based index for the first beetle in the fight file.

animal1.label: Animal 1 index
animal1.help: Zero-based index for the second beetle in the fight file.

contact_px.label: Contact/proximity threshold, pixels
contact_px.help: Pairwise distance threshold for contact/proximity events.

fight_px.label: Possible fight threshold, pixels
fight_px.help: Very-close pairwise distance threshold for candidate fight-like events.

min_fight_frames.label: Minimum fight-like duration, frames
min_fight_frames.help: Minimum duration for candidate fight-like close-contact events.

move_threshold_px.label: Movement threshold, pixels
move_threshold_px.help: Individual displacement/cumulative movement threshold used for BA-style movement onset metrics.

movement_onset_consecutive_frames.label: Sustained movement frames
movement_onset_consecutive_frames.help: Number of consecutive frames above movement threshold for sustained movement onset.

max_step_px.label: Artifact jump threshold, pixels
max_step_px.help: One-frame jumps larger than this are treated as likely artifacts and interpolated if possible.

roi_wall_buffer_px.label: ROI wall buffer, pixels
roi_wall_buffer_px.help: Distance from primary ROI wall used to classify border occupancy.

turtling_window_frames.label: Turtling scan window, frames
turtling_window_frames.help: Window size, in tracking frames, for centroid-based turtling-like detection. In 1 fps timelapse data, 300 frames is about 300 seconds.

turtling_min_duration_frames.label: Minimum turtling duration, frames
turtling_min_duration_frames.help: Minimum run length, in tracking frames, required to retain a turtling-like event. In 1 fps timelapse data, 300 frames is about 300 seconds.

metadata_notes: Analysis start frame is read from each cell TOML and stored as toml_analysis_start_frame in the manifest.
